_comment: TRUE POSITIVE — SP-101001: Registry Run Key persistence (T1547.001)
scenario: Malware writes a new value to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run to persist across user logins
why_tp: New Run key value written by a non-system process from AppData\Roaming
Binary name 'svchost32.exe' masquerades as Windows svchost.exe
Process writing the key IS the same binary being persisted (self-installation)
AppData\Roaming path — writable by user, common malware staging location
analyst_action: Hash svchost32.exe and submit to VirusTotal / sandbox
Check process tree: how was svchost32.exe launched initially?
Remove the Run key value and terminate the process if malicious
